frameworks.name: frequency-band decomposition of LFP
frameworks.one_line: separating local field potential into non-overlapping bands as distinct neural signal components

frameworks.name: BOLD-LFP coupling
frameworks.one_line: linking hemodynamic signal to band-specific electrophysiological activity

frameworks.name: information theory
frameworks.one_line: characterizing statistical dependency between BOLD and LFP bands

core_question: Does the BOLD signal reflect each LFP band independently, or does it also encode relationships among different bands?
key_assumptions: LFP components can be partly separated into non-overlapping frequency bands
BOLD-LFP statistical dependency can be quantified with information theory
spontaneous activity in visual cortex is informative about coupling structure
manipulations: simultaneous BOLD and LFP recording during spontaneous activity
comparison of single-band vs cross-band information about BOLD
key_findings: alpha, beta, and gamma LFP bands all carry information about BOLD
gamma is the most informative band, with BOLD tracking gamma power increases and decreases
alpha and beta carry information about BOLD complementary to gamma
alpha-gamma relation maps onto BOLD amplitude; beta-gamma relation maps onto BOLD latency
interpretations: BOLD reflects not just individual LFP bands but specific cross-band relationships
amplitude and timing of BOLD encode distinct aspects of multi-band LFP structure
